headlines: US Stocks closed firmer, driven by mega-cap names (MSFT, TSLA, META) ahead of earnings
Dollar regains some poise after slumping on 'de-dollarisation' theme
Trump threatens 25% tariffs on Canada & South Korea (Autos, Pharma)
marketFocus.macroContext: Markets are stabilizing in neutral territory ahead of the critical FOMC Rate Decision and Mag-7 earnings. Risk sentiment is cautious but balanced. 'De-dollarisation' remains a key narrative, but USD is seeing a tactical recovery.

tradingSummary: Focus shifts entirely to the FOMC meeting tonight. USD is in a holding pattern with a slight bullish recovery bias. GBP shows relative strength, testing yearly highs. CAD is under heavy pressure from Trump's tariff threats. JPY weakens despite hawkish BoJ minutes.

USD.headline: FOMC tonight – positioning squeeze
USD.summary: Fed holds at 4.25-4.50% (priced). Powell's tone on inflation/tariffs matters. Real yields steady, 2Y at 4.25%. 'De-dollarisation' narrative fading—short covering into event. Tactical bounce 1-2 days, then reassess post-Powell.

EUR.headline: ECB jawboning caps rally
EUR.summary: Inflation sticky at 2.5%, but growth weak. ECB's Kocher warns on FX strength—verbal intervention risk above 1.05. Bund yields flat, no rate cut urgency. Holding gains, but topside limited by CB pushback. Watch 1.0450-1.0500 zone.

GBP.headline: BoE hawkish, data solid
GBP.summary: UK inflation above target (3.0%), BoE cuts priced out. Gilt yields rising (4.60% 10Y)—rate differential favors GBP. Domestic demand resilient. Testing 1.3788 highs—break targets 1.3850. Strongest G10 fundamentals short-term.

JPY.headline: Carry flows trump BoJ hawkishness
JPY.summary: BoJ minutes showed 'appropriate to raise rates'—market ignores it. JGB 10Y at 1.25%, real yields negative. Ueda's credibility issue—no urgency priced. Trump FX comments add noise. Carry unwind risk exists, but flows still short JPY. Bias weak 1-3 days.

AUD.headline: CPI in-line, China stimulus wait
AUD.summary: Q4 CPI 3.6% (expected)—RBA holds in Feb (priced). Iron ore $100/t stable, but China PMI soft. Copper flat. No domestic catalyst until RBA Feb 18. China policy hopes keep floor under AUD, but no breakout driver. Range-bound 1-3 days.

CAD.headline: Trump tariff threat + BoC cut risk
CAD.summary: Trump threatens 25% auto/pharma tariffs—direct hit to CAD. BoC decision today: 50bp cut expected (3.75% to 3.25%). Oil $74 WTI—no support from energy. Macklem dovish, growth concerns. Weakest G10 fundamentals. Sell rallies 1-3 days.

CHF.headline: No independent drivers
CHF.summary: SNB last cut Dec, next meeting March. Inflation 0.6%—well below target. No flight-to-safety flows despite geopolitical noise. CHF tracks EUR moves mechanically. No CB rhetoric, no data. Dead money 1-3 days—avoid unless hedging.

NZD.headline: RBNZ dovish, dairy prices soft
NZD.summary: RBNZ cut 50bp Jan 22 (4.25%)—more cuts priced (3.50% by mid-year). Dairy auction prices -1.2%—headwind for NZD. No domestic data until Feb. China stimulus hopes provide only support. Follows AUD/risk sentiment. No edge 1-3 days.

topTrades.reason: Divergence: Strongest G10 (GBP) vs Weakest G10 (CAD) on Tariff Threats
Recovery Play: USD stabilizing ahead of FOMC vs JPY weakness despite BoJ
Tariff Risk: Euro resilience vs Canadian Dollar vulnerability
